description: 50 production-ready AI prompts you can copy, paste, and use today — no setup, no learning curve, no AI slop.

prompts.name: LinkedIn InMail cold
prompts.prompt_text: 120-word LinkedIn message to a {TITLE} at {COMPANY_TYPE}. One genuine reason you are reaching out specifically. One open question about their situation. Purpose: start a conversation, not book a call.

prompts.name: LinkedIn operator post
prompts.prompt_text: Write a LinkedIn post from the POV of a founder who turned {COMPANY} from zero to ${REVENUE} in {TIME}. No bragging. One counterintuitive insight per short paragraph. 3-4 paragraphs total. End with one question. No emojis, no hashtags.

prompts.name: TikTok hook-first caption
prompts.prompt_text: First sentence: a hook that makes someone stop scrolling. Second sentence: the payoff/insight about {TOPIC}. Max 200 chars. No emojis, no hashtags. Format: [hook] — [insight about {TOPIC}].

prompts.name: Claude summarisation system prompt
prompts.prompt_text: Claude system prompt for summarizing {CONTENT_TYPE} documents. Output format: TL;DR (one sentence) → 3-5 key points → one analysis sentence. Neutral expert tone. No advice. Specifies response language rules.

prompts.name: Customer service bot system prompt
prompts.prompt_text: System prompt for a {COMPANY_TYPE} customer service chatbot. Rules: be concise, offer specific next steps, flag human handoff. Warm tone, no friendliness theatre. <200 words. Include escalation triggers.

prompts.name: Tool-routing agent system prompt
prompts.prompt_text: System prompt for an AI assistant routing to 3 tools: {T1}, {T2}, {T3}. Rules: identify intent, select right tool, never invent data. Output JSON schema included.

prompts.name: OpenAPI endpoint YAML
prompts.prompt_text: 20-line OpenAPI 3.0 YAML snippet for POST endpoint: {ENDPOINT}. One request body field, 201 response struct, Bearer security. Valid YAML.

prompts.name: 15 checkout CTA labels
prompts.prompt_text: 15 checkout CTA button labels for a digital product. Mix clarity, urgency, and aspirational framing. Each 1-5 words. Flag any that are weak and why they do not convert.

prompts.name: Code review feedback
prompts.prompt_text: Review a PR changing {COMPONENT} in {TECH}. Name 2 strengths, suggest 2 improvements (one performance, one readability). 80 words. Constructive tone.

prompts.name: DALL-E brand illustration
prompts.prompt_text: DALL-E prompt for flat-vector brand illustration of {SUBJECT} for a {INDUSTRY} brand. Must read clearly at 512px thumbnail. No photorealism, no text, no logos.

prompts.name: Website hero image
prompts.prompt_text: 16:9 website hero image prompt for a {NICHE} brand. Brand colors: {C1} + {C2}. Subject: {SUBJECT}. Mood: {MOOD}. No text or logos. DALL-E 3 format: one paragraph, all details.

prompts.name: Weighted decision matrix
prompts.prompt_text: Choose between {OPT_A} and {OPT_B} for {DECISION}. Weighted decision matrix: 4 criteria with weights, score each option out of 10 per criterion, final recommendation with one-line reasoning. 150 words max.

prompts.name: Discord community announcement
prompts.prompt_text: Discord announcement message to your community: {ANNOUNCEMENT}. Casual first-person, 'heads up' energy. What changes, what you are asking, one bottom-line question. Under 100 words.

prompts.name: 5 email subject lines
prompts.prompt_text: Five email subject lines for a '{TOPIC}' newsletter. Each targets a different persuasion lever: (1) curiosity gap, (2) FOMO clock, (3) social proof, (4) low-effort win, (5) contrarian hook. Label each.

prompts.name: Bad news email to customer
prompts.prompt_text: Inform a customer that {BAD_NEWS} regarding {PROJECT}. Age it well: delay a sentence, clarity, a direct yes/no, then what replaces it. Warm but not effuse. 100 words.

prompts.name: Cold email opener
prompts.prompt_text: Cold email opener for reaching a {TITLE} at a {COMPANY_TYPE}. Never mention the product. One specific signal you researched them. Max 22 words.

prompts.name: Email-to-text reframe for busy execs
prompts.prompt_text: Rewrite the 120-word version of {CONTENT_SUBJECT} for a C-level exec who checks email on mobile in <15 seconds. Frontload: decision needed, then one-line rationale, then what happens if nothing changes. 80 words total.

prompts.name: Launch email sequence
prompts.prompt_text: 3 product launch emails, 2 days apart. Email 1: teaser → build → waitlist CTA. Email 2: launch → proof → purchase CTA. Email 3: reminder + deadline urgency. 80-120 words each.

prompts.name: Newsletter intro
prompts.prompt_text: Opening paragraph of a solo-founder newsletter on {TOPIC}. 40 words. Peer-update feel, not a broadcast. Specific, warm, teases content without revealing all.

prompts.name: Post-purchase welcome email
prompts.prompt_text: Welcome email after purchase of {PRODUCT}. 90 words. Confirm purchase → two-step starter action → one support detail → sign off.

prompts.name: Re-engagement email
prompts.prompt_text: Re-engagement email for subscribers inactive 90 days. Self-deprecating about oversending. One related exclusive resource as incentive. No guilt trips. 80 words.

prompts.name: Status update email
prompts.prompt_text: Status update for a feature incident: {FEATURE}. 3 paragraphs max: what happened, what is resolved, ETA. Simple words. No jargon. No defensive language. One genuine apology.

prompts.name: Facebook ad copy
prompts.prompt_text: 150-word Facebook ad for {PRODUCT}. Structure: [2 sec hook] → [pain] → [solution] → [proof in metrics] → [CTA]. One benefit per sentence. One clear call to action.

prompts.name: Conventional commit message
prompts.prompt_text: Conventional commit for change: {CHANGE}. Format: <type>(<scope>): subject (≤50 chars) + empty line + body (≤72 chars/line, explains what and why, not how). Type: {feat|fix|refactor|docs}.

prompts.name: IG Stories 5-card swipe sequence
prompts.prompt_text: 5 consecutive Instagram Stories slides about {TOPIC}. One sentence each. Progressive build: hook → 2 learning → pain → CTA. Under 40 words per slide. No emojis.

prompts.name: IG carousel slide 1 hook
prompts.prompt_text: Slide 1 of a 10-slide Instagram carousel about {TOPIC}. Hook must interrupt scroll. One dominant number or fact. No exclamation points. Under 20 words on this slide.

prompts.name: Beta announcement arc
prompts.prompt_text: 320-word product launch announcement arc for beta of {PRODUCT}. Opening hook with the pain in direct plain language. Problem context (1-2 para). Beta decision with one genuine tension. Close with a specific call for the first 50 beta testers to contact you.

prompts.name: LinkedIn carousel body copy cheat sheet
prompts.prompt_text: Contextual copy for a 7-slide Instagram carousel on {TOPIC}: Slide1 hook, Slide2 pain, Slides3-6 evidence or examples, Slide7 CTA. Max 280 chars per slide body. Label each slide.

prompts.name: Midjourney product photo
prompts.prompt_text: Midjourney v6 prompt for product photography of {PRODUCT}. Neutral studio lighting, {BG_COLOR} background, centered, catalogue quality. --v 6 --ar 3:2 --style raw. Format: subject → composition → tech parameters.

prompts.name: Hostile audience opening
prompts.prompt_text: Opening line for a talk to an audience that is already skeptical about {TOPIC}. Do not defend, do not apologize. Name the doubt directly, then reframe it as your core argument. 35 words. Wins the room before it starts.

prompts.name: Five whys root cause
prompts.prompt_text: Team at {COMPANY_NICHE} facing '{PROBLEM}'. Run the Five Whys with actual answers (not generic). End with one mandatory action. 120 words. No jargon.

prompts.name: Internal product brief template
prompts.prompt_text: Write a 5-sentence interal product brief for {FEATURE}. Include: the user pain, the proposed solution shape, what success looks like in 30 days. Sharp, no fluff. Sprint-planning ready.

prompts.name: 5 quick wins list
prompts.prompt_text: 5 quick wins a {ROLE} at a {STAGE} startup can implement this week on {TOPIC}. Each: one sentence, no budget, under 2 hours. Total 8 lines.

prompts.name: SaaS landing H1 + subhead
prompts.prompt_text: H1 (≤7 words of benefit) + subhead (≤15 words decoding the mechanism) for a SaaS page selling {PRODUCT} to {AUDIENCE}. Benefit-first, not feature-first. No jargon.

prompts.name: Objection reframe
prompts.prompt_text: Objection: '{OBJECTION}' when selling {PRODUCT}. Feel-Felt-Found reply in 60 words. Acknowledge → align → outcome evidence. No defensiveness.

prompts.name: Blog pillar article outline
prompts.prompt_text: 6-section H2 outline for 1800-word pillar article: {TITLE}. Each: heading + 2 sub-bullets + one key idea. Intro + conclusion included.

prompts.name: FAQ section
prompts.prompt_text: 5 FAQ entries for {PRODUCT}. Two pre-purchase, two objections, one logistics. Format: **Q:** | A: 40-60 words. Include section heading.

prompts.name: Listicle intro
prompts.prompt_text: 60-word intro for a listicle: {TITLE}. First sentence: the problem. Second: tease the sharpest insight. Include the action verb {VERB} in the hook sentence.

prompts.name: SEO meta description
prompts.prompt_text: SEO meta description for a blog post about {TOPIC} targeting keyword {KEYWORD}. Max 160 chars. Natural fit. End with 'Learn more'.

prompts.name: Heckler call-out reply
prompts.prompt_text: On a LinkedIn post about {TOPIC}, someone says 'That only works if you are lucky / have connections.' Write a confident, generous, data-backed reply. 60-80 words. Wins the thread.

prompts.name: SQL query explainer
prompts.prompt_text: Explain SQL query in plain words for a non-technical stakeholder: {QUERY}. Break down: what it reads | what it filters | business output. 120 words max.

prompts.name: Subversive one-liner tweet
prompts.prompt_text: One tweet quietly challenging a dominant belief in {NICHE}. Feels like on-the-ground context, not a hot take. Max 280 chars.

prompts.name: Twitter reply that wins the thread
prompts.prompt_text: Respond to a viral tweet about {TOPIC}. Add one data point the OP missed. 2-3 sentences. End with a question that continues the conversation.

prompts.name: Twitter thread opener
prompts.prompt_text: Write a 7-tweet thread about {TOPIC}. Tweet 1: counterintuitive but defensible. Tweets 2-6: one point + one example per tweet. Tweet 7: specific CTA. Zero filler.

prompts.name: YouTube Shorts 15s script
prompts.prompt_text: 15-second YouTube Shorts script about {TOPIC}. Format: 0-4s hook | 4-12s main point with visual cue | 12-15s CTA. Script in seconds. Total words under 70.

prompts.name: YouTube comment reply as creator
prompts.prompt_text: Reply to a YouTube comment on a {TOPIC} video. 30-50 words. Answer the question, add one tip not in the video, invite deeper questions.

prompts.name: YouTube description + chapters
prompts.prompt_text: YouTube description for a 15-min video on {TOPIC}. 2-paragraph hook, 5 named chapter timestamps at approximate positions, 3 key takeaways as bullets. End CTA: subscribe + comment code word.

prompts.name: YouTube thumbnail concept
prompts.prompt_text: YouTube thumbnail prompt for video: {TITLE}. Max 3 visual elements, one high-contrast dominant focal subject, red or orange accent. No text in image. --ar 16:9.
